blocks.content: Daisy's dorm was a brick building on the east side of campus that smelled like old wood and radiator heat. Her roommate, Claire, had string lights already up and a corkboard full of photos from home and a genuinely warm smile that made Daisy feel like she'd landed somewhere safe.

blocks.speaker: Daisy
blocks.content: Georgia. Small town. You've never heard of it.

blocks.speaker: Claire
blocks.content: I'm from a small town in Iowa. I probably get it.

blocks.speaker: Claire
blocks.content: You're right. Never heard of it. But I like the name.
Minnesota was green still in August but she could feel the cold underneath it. The campus was enormous and quiet.
But at night, in the unfamiliar dark, she reached for her phone.
yeah. darius is asleep already. guy goes to bed at 10
it's a lot. good a lot but a lot a lot. how are you
same. my roommate is nice. the campus is beautiful. i miss home more than i thought i would
